belief_updates.name: Overseas Manufacturing Is a Structural Short in Tariff Regimes
belief_updates.description: When a sustained tariff regime targets manufacturing countries (Vietnam, Indonesia, China), companies with heavy production exposure in those regions face dual compression: input cost increases that erode margin if absorbed, or demand destruction if passed through to consumers. This materializes in actual earnings deterioration over 1-3 quarters. Entry screen: heavy Asia-Pacific manufacturing + consumer discretionary or brand-dependent revenue + falling/flat OBV + thin margins (<10%). The edge is entering before earnings confirmation, using OBV distribution as the early signal. NKE (+12.6% profit) and SBUX (Q2 confirmed) are the canonical validation trades. The thesis is NOT invalidated by a tariff pause on non-relevant countries — only direct relief on the specific manufacturing country matters.

belief_updates.name: Hold High-ADX Shorts Through Policy-Driven Bounces
belief_updates.description: When a strong-trending short position (ADX > 35 at entry) experiences a 1-3 day bounce driven by a policy or earnings catalyst, the correct response is thesis validation, not price-based exit. Checklist: (1) Is the bounce directly relevant to THIS company's specific thesis driver? If no, it is noise. (2) Is OBV still flat or falling? If yes, no institutional reversal is occurring. (3) Is ADX still elevated? If yes, primary trend intact. Capitulating on high-conviction shorts during bounces is the primary error in bear market execution — the short-covering itself is evidence the trend is intact. SBUX ADX=39 through Q2 bounce is the canonical example.

lessons: When shorting a company on a product-specific impairment thesis (export controls, product recall, regulatory action on one product line), verify that the company cannot redirect revenue through alternative products or markets before entering. NVDA's H20 export control thesis failed because NVDA has demonstrated ability to redirect AI sales through compliant alternatives — the market priced in management's guidance over the structural headwind. Trigger: Before entering a 'product-specific impairment' short, ask: 'Does management have a credible alternative revenue path, and has the stock already partially priced in the impairment?' If yes, reduce allocation (max 5%) and tighten stop to 8% from entry. Companies with dominant market position and pricing power tend to find workarounds faster than policy-only short theses assume.
